title: Bilabrella thomsonii
intro: Bilabrella thomsonii, commonly known as Bilabrella, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its distinctive bilabiate flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Bilabrella thomsonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Bilabrella is traditionally used in herbal medicine and is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestion and relieve gastrointestinal discomfort.

uses: Bilabrella thomsonii has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions.
